search:docker-kubernetes-logging-elastic.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `kubectl apply -f <filename>.yaml` to deploy resources.
  - `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces` to list all pods across namespaces.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Elasticsearch: v8.x
  - Kibana: v8.x
  - Fluentd: v1.14.x

- **Key Config Options**:
  - Elasticsearch: `discovery.type: single-node`
  - Kibana: `elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://<elasticsearch-host>:9200"]`
  - Fluentd: `elasticsearch.host => "<elasticsearch-host>"`

- **Fluentd Docker Image**: `fluent/fluentd-kubernetes-daemonset:v2.5.2-debian-elasticsearch7-1`
- **Elasticsearch Deployment**:
  - Command: `kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/kubernetes/elasticsearch.yaml`
  - Key Config: `es.cluster.name`, `es.node.name`, `discovery.seed_hosts`, `cluster.initial_master_nodes`
- **Kibana Deployment**:
  - Command: `kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/kubernetes/kibana.yaml`
  - Key Config: `elasticsearch.url`
- **Fluentd Configuration**:
  - File Path: `/fluentd/etc/fluent.conf`
  - Key Sections: `<source>`, `<match>`, `<filter>`
- **Elasticsearch Service**:
  - Command: `kubectl expose deployment elasticsearch --type=NodePort --port=9200`
  - Key Config: `node.port`
